description: This figure presents X-ray diffraction (XRD) analysis results of Fe-28Mn-8Al-1C low-density steel subjected to cryogenic rolling (CR) at deformation levels of 0% (undeformed), 30%, 50%, 70%, and 90%. Panel (a) displays the XRD patterns showing single-phase austenite (γ) structure with indexed peaks γ(111), γ(200), γ(220), and γ(311), where progressive peak broadening occurs with increasing deformation without martensitic transformation. Panel (b) quantifies the full width at half maximum (FWHM) of the γ(220) peak, which increases linearly from 0.496° in the undeformed state to 1.022° after 90% CR. Panel (c) shows the calculated microstrain (black squares) and dislocation density (red triangles) derived from Williamson-Hall analysis: microstrain rises from 2.8×10⁻³ to 7×10⁻³, while dislocation density increases from 3.2×10¹⁴ m⁻² to 23.6×10¹⁴ m⁻², with accelerated growth at higher deformation levels. These data demonstrate that cryogenic rolling induces significant lattice distortion and dislocation accumulation while maintaining the austenitic phase stability.
